description: 47 - 27-11-2024 - Emails from Peter Mandelson (whilst at Global Counsel) to Sarah Jones (former Minister of State for Industry)

(former Minister of State for Industry)

From: Minister JONES (DBT) Personal
Sent on: Wednesday, November 27, 2024 6:13:09 PM

ce: Third Party
Subject: RE: Invitation to roundtable

Hi Third
Party
Thanks so much for your follow up.

We are still looking into this and will consider those further dates in our considerations.
Very best wishes and thanks

Jcs
JCS Private Secretary to the Minister of State for Industry (DESNZ and DBT)

Department for Business and Trade | Old Admiralty Building,
Personal

Personal

From: Third Party

Sent: 12 November 2024 12:24

To: Minister JONES (DBT) ; JCS
Ce: Third Party

Subject: RE: Invitation to roundtable

Hi JCS

Thank you for confirming the invitation is still being considered.

As the 10th/11th of December are now relatively close we are considering moving the event
to January to allow for a longer lead in time.

We were wondering whether the minister might be available on any of the following dates:

O Monday 13th of January
(0 Tuesday 14th January
(0 Monday 20th January
(0 Tuesday 21st January
In terms of timing, we would still be looking at late afternoon (votes permitting).

Many thanks,
Third
Party
Global Counsel | Senior Associate
Personal

Subject: RE: Invitation to roundtable 
Hi 
, 
I hope you are well. 
I was wondering whether there were any updates on Lord Mandelson’ s invitation for the 
minister to speak at a private roundtable focussing on how the Clean Energy Mission 
can be an important lever for building out our industrial strategy. 
As highlighted below, we are looking at late afternoon (votes permitting) on Tuesday 10th or 
Wednesday 11th of December, but we can come back with some alternative dates if this 
doesn’t work. We do hope the minister can join us. 
Please let me know if you have any questions regarding the event. 
Kind regards, 
Global Counsel | Senior Associate

I was wondering if the minister has had time to consider Lord Mandelson’ s invitation to

Please do let me know if you have any questions about the event or if I can help with

Best wishes, 
 
 
Global Counsel | Senior Associate

From: 
 
Sent: 26 September 2024 11:20 
To: Minister JONES (DBT)
 
Cc:
 
 
Subject: RE: Invitation to roundtable 
Dear Sarah Jones MP, 
I hope you are well. 
Lord Mandelson has asked me to follow up with the invitation below to join a roundtable 
discussion. 
Please do let me know if you have any questions regarding the event or if I can help with 
anything. 
Kind regards, 
 
Global Counsel | Senior Executive Assistant 
 
From: Peter Mandelson 
 
Sent: 13 September 2024 13:05 
To:
 
Cc:
 
 
Subject: Invitation to roundtable 
Dear Sarah, 
I hope you are well and settling into your important new role. 
I am planning to reschedule the roundtable you agreed to participate in just before the 
election was called - this time focussing on how the Clean Energy Mission can be an 
important lever for building out our industrial strategy . And I wondered if you would be 
willing and available to lead off a discussion on the topic? SSE will also give some initial 
remarks. 
A number of industry people have highlighted to me that the new government's more 
ambitious decarbonisation agenda presents a major economic opportunity to onshore cable 
manufacturing, leverage the UK's geographical strengths in carbon capture and repurpose 
the UK's ports for offshore wind turbine making . The challenge will be identifying where the 
UK can compete with its international counterparts on green manufacturing and ensuring 
that policy and regulation choices develop these opportunities. In the absence of this, the UK 
risks not fully capture the benefits of decarbonisation. 
I am keen to bring together a group of interesting and, hopefully, lively people to discuss this 
opportunity and challenge. I would plan to invite a mix of industry, Labour MPs and policy 
people, senior union people and relevant influencers for what would be a Chatham House 
rule discussion. 
Personal
Personal
Personal
Third Party
Third Party
Third 
Party
Third Party
Personal
161


And of course, would be open to shaping the conversation according to your current focus in 
the departments. 
I would be pleased to speak with you about if further if useful. We are potentially looking at 
late afternoon (votes permitting) on Tuesday 10th or Wednesday 11th December – might 
either work with your diary? My office can come back with some alternatives if not. 
I very much hope you can do this, and it would be great to hear what you have to say. 
Very best, 
Peter 
Lord Mandelson 
President and Co-Founder
